author: vaiṣṇava⋅dāsa
verses:
viṣaye sakale matta, nāhi kṛṣṇa-nāma⋅tattva,
bhaktiśūnya hoila avanī
kalikāla⋅sarpaviṣe, dagdha jīva mithyā⋅rase,
nā jānaye kebā se āpani

nija kanyā⋅putrotsave, mātiyā āchaye sabe,
nāhi anya śubha karmaleśa
jakṣa pūje madya⋅māṁse, nānārūpa jīva hiṁse,
ei mata haila sarva-deśa

dekhiyā karuṇā kari’, ‘kama-lākṣa’ nāma dhari’,
avatīrṇa hailā gauḍadeśe
vraja⋅rāja⋅kumāra, sāṅgopāṅga avatāra,
karāiba ei abhilāṣe

sarva āge āguyāna, jīvere kariyā trāṇa,
śāntipure hoilā prakāśa
sakala duṣkṛti jā’be, sabe kṛṣṇa-nāma pā’be,
kahe dīna vaiṣṇavera dāsa
en_translation:
Everyone is intoxicated by material sense objects and knows nothing of the truth of the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa. The entire world has become devoid of devotion. Bitten by the poisonous snake of Kali-yuga, living entities are scorched by the false tastes of illusion, and they do not even know who they really are.

Everyone is busy celebrating the festivals of their own sons and daughters, and there is not even a trace of any other auspicious activity. They worship demons with meat and wine and commit violence against various living beings. This is the condition throughout the land.

Seeing this and feeling great compassion, Lord Kṛṣṇa, assuming the name 'Kamalākṣa' (the Lotus-eyed One), descended in the land of Gauḍa. His only desire was to cause the Prince of Vraja to incarnate along with all His associates.

Taking the lead before everyone else to deliver the living entities, He manifested Himself in Śāntipura. Vaiṣṇava Dāsa says: "All sins will now be destroyed, and everyone will receive the Holy Name of Kṛṣṇa."
